title: Bulk report - Week 20
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market managed to turn its fortunes around this week stemming the slide from the previous week. Opening the week at $34,542 the 5TC weakened until midweek before posting a solid 959 gain to settle at $32,593. This revival is largely Pacific led with a small assist from the ballaster routes.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market managed to turn its fortunes around this week stemming the slide from the previous week. Opening the week at $34,542 the 5TC weakened until midweek before posting a solid 959 gain to settle at $32,593.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The start of the week proved to be a little muted for Panamaxes with mixed opinions on the market with consistently wide bid/offer spreads. The Atlantic saw solid support for the front haul trips from the North Americas, whilst transatlantic demand proved to be weak with rates driven down particularly for the short duration trips.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: Overall a more positive week for the sector, as more enquiry appeared from the Atlantic giving rise to stronger numbers being available in key areas whilst there remained strong demand from Asia. The BSI saw a week-on-week jump of 111 points, closing at 2,408.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: In a week which has seen both the Atlantic and Asia markets have limited activity, the BHSI has remained positive this week. Charterers continue to take tonnage in both basins for period, which suggests that some see a firm market for the foreseeable future.
